ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

Source candidates for the Quantum Computing Researcher position (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) from all available channels.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Source candidates for the Quantum Computing Researcher position (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) from all available channels.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. Job Title: Quantum Computing Researcher
2. Job ID: JOB-NICHE-2025-001

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. Job posting details including status (open or closed).
2. Specific requirements for the Quantum Computing Researcher position, including must-have and nice-to-have skills.
3. Salary range for the position.
4. Location requirements for the role.
5. Any special requirements such as clearance or certifications.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. Urgency level or time-to-fill targets based on company standards or similar roles.
2. Ideal candidate profile based on industry standards for Quantum Computing Researchers.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates may need a strong background in quantum physics and computer science.
2. The role likely requires proficiency in quantum programming languages or frameworks.
3. Candidates may be sourced from academic institutions, tech companies, or research labs specializing in quantum computing.

Fantastic. To address this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- TalentScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

Based on the team composition, and known and unknown facts, please devise a short bullet-point plan for addressing the
original request. Remember, there is no requirement to involve all team members. A team member's particular expertise
may not be needed for this task.

- **ReqMaster**:
  - Validate the job ID (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) to confirm it is open and approved for sourcing.
  - Extract detailed job requirements, including must-have and nice-to-have skills.
  - Identify any special requirements (e.g., certifications, security clearance) associated with the position.
  
- **TalentScout**:
  - Begin sourcing candidates from relevant channels such as academic institutions, conferences, and online platforms specializing in quantum computing.
  - Utilize social media and professional networks to reach potential candidates in the quantum computing field.
  - Leverage existing databases and recruitment platforms to match candidates with extracted job requirements.

Source candidates for the Quantum Computing Researcher position (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) from all available channels.


To answer this request we have assembled the following team:

- TalentScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>


Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. Job Title: Quantum Computing Researcher
2. Job ID: JOB-NICHE-2025-001

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. Job posting details including status (open or closed).
2. Specific requirements for the Quantum Computing Researcher position, including must-have and nice-to-have skills.
3. Salary range for the position.
4. Location requirements for the role.
5. Any special requirements such as clearance or certifications.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. Urgency level or time-to-fill targets based on company standards or similar roles.
2. Ideal candidate profile based on industry standards for Quantum Computing Researchers.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates may need a strong background in quantum physics and computer science.
2. The role likely requires proficiency in quantum programming languages or frameworks.
3. Candidates may be sourced from academic institutions, tech companies, or research labs specializing in quantum computing.


Here is the plan to follow as best as possible:

- **ReqMaster**:
  - Validate the job ID (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) to confirm it is open and approved for sourcing.
  - Extract detailed job requirements, including must-have and nice-to-have skills.
  - Identify any special requirements (e.g., certifications, security clearance) associated with the position.
  
- **TalentScout**:
  - Begin sourcing candidates from relevant channels such as academic institutions, conferences, and online platforms specializing in quantum computing.
  - Utilize social media and professional networks to reach potential candidates in the quantum computing field.
  - Leverage existing databases and recruitment platforms to match candidates with extracted job requirements.

Recall we are working on the following request:

Source candidates for the Quantum Computing Researcher position (JOB-NICHE-2025-001) from all available channels.

And we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- TalentScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

To make progress on the request, please answer the following questions, including necessary reasoning:

    - Is the request fully satisfied? (True if complete, or False if the original request has yet to be
      SUCCESSFULLY and FULLY addressed)
    - Are we in a loop where we are repeating the same requests and or getting the same responses as before?
      Loops can span multiple turns, and can include repeated actions like scrolling up or down more than a
      handful of times.
    - Are we making forward progress? (True if just starting, or recent messages are adding value. False if recent
      messages show evidence of being stuck in a loop or if there is evidence of significant barriers to success
      such as the inability to read from a required file)
    - Who should speak next? (select from: ReqMaster, TalentScout)
    - What instruction or question would you give this team member? (Phrase as if speaking directly to them, and
      include any specific information they may need)

Please output an answer in pure JSON format according to the following schema. The JSON object must be parsable as-is.
DO NOT OUTPUT ANYTHING OTHER THAN JSON, AND DO NOT DEVIATE FROM THIS SCHEMA:

{
    "is_request_satisfied": {

        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "is_in_loop": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "is_progress_being_made": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "next_speaker": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": string (select from: ReqMaster, TalentScout)
    },
    "instruction_or_question": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": string
    }
}

parts.response:
Structured Requirements for JOB-NICHE-2025-001 (Quantum Computing Research Scientist):

MUST HAVE:
PhD in Quantum Physics, 10+ years research, Published papers

NICE TO HAVE:
None specified

Minimum Salary: $200,000
Location Requirement: Redmond, WA (On-site required)

system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

parts.content:
Here’s an updated fact sheet for the Quantum Computing Researcher position (JOB-NICHE-2025-001), incorporating inferred details based on the structured requirements extracted and reasoning for educated guesses:

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. **Job Title:** Quantum Computing Researcher
2. **Job ID:** JOB-NICHE-2025-001
3. **Must-Have Skills:** 
   - PhD in Quantum Physics
   - 10+ years of research experience
   - Published research papers

4. **Salary Range:** Minimum salary of $200,000
5. **Location Requirement:** On-site in Redmond, WA

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. Confirm job posting details including current status (open or closed).
2. Explore additional requirements, if any, for the Quantum Computing Researcher position, especially regarding certifications or specific programming skills.
3. Identify any urgency level or time-to-fill targets based on company hiring metrics.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. **Urgency Level:** Given the specialized skill set required and the high salary, it’s likely the urgency level is **high**, meaning it may be critical to fill this role quickly.
2. **Ideal Candidate Profile:** Candidates should not only possess advanced degrees and extensive experience but also demonstrate effective communication skills for collaboration within research teams, which is essential in a scientific environment.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. **Background Requirements:** Candidates likely need a solid foundation in both quantum physics and advanced mathematics, potentially alongside experience in quantum information science.
2. **Programming Skills:** The role likely requires proficiency in quantum programming languages or frameworks, with specific languages (such as Qiskit or Cirq) being beneficial.
3. **Candidate Sourcing:** Target candidates may include those from leading academic institutions, tech companies making advancements in quantum technologies, or research labs specializing in quantum innovation. 

These updates aim to provide a comprehensive view of the requirements and expectations associated with sourcing candidates for this critical position.

Please briefly explain what went wrong on this last run
(the root cause of the failure), and then come up with a new plan that takes steps and includes hints to overcome prior
challenges and especially avoids repeating the same mistakes. As before, the new plan should be concise, expressed in
bullet-point form, and consider the following team composition:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- TalentScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

### Root Cause of Failure
The last attempt to source candidates for the Quantum Computing Researcher position did not yield the desired progress because:
- **Inadequate Validation:** The job posting details, including the status, were not confirmed, leading to assumptions based on outdated information.
- **Lack of Comprehensive Skills Analysis:** Specific must-have and nice-to-have skills were not fully extracted, limiting the ability to target appropriate candidates.
- **Undefined Urgency and Profile:** Not establishing a clear urgency level and an ideal candidate profile hampered the ability to create a focused sourcing strategy.

### New Plan
- **ReqMaster:**
  - **Validate Job Posting:** Confirm the status of JOB-NICHE-2025-001 and any additional details before proceeding.
  - **Extract Detailed Requirements:** Utilize the tools available to pull specific must-have and nice-to-have skills, as well as any certifications required for the role.
- **TalentScout:**
  - **Develop Targeted Sourcing Strategy:** Based on the confirmed requirements from ReqMaster, identify potential candidate sources (academic institutions, tech companies, research labs).
  - **Profile and Urgency Identification:** Collaborate with ReqMaster to define an ideal candidate profile and urgent hiring timeline to focus efforts more effectively.
- **Collaboration Steps:**
  - Schedule a regular check-in to reassess the progress and adapt strategies based on initial outreach outcomes.
  - Discuss candidate feedback to refine sourcing methods iteratively, ensuring alignment with the defined profile. 

This plan emphasizes validation and collaboration, aiming to avoid previous pitfalls and streamline the candidate sourcing process.
